title: Where the Grid Flattens into Silence

features.name: a wireframe dissolving skyward
features.desc-on-touch: A grid that is slowly forgetting how to hold shape. It was made to organize movement, but the road has learned that stillness is a kind of travel too. The wireframe is fading, becoming sky.

features.name: a glyph-mark where grass and tar debate
features.desc-on-touch: A plaque at the border where the grid becomes field. The inscription is both route-marker and weed. Neither the highway nor the paddock claims it, but both have written on it.

features.name: a pool where sound becomes absence
features.desc-on-touch: Wind moves here without the engine's countervoice. The pool is empty, but it remembers the sound of every vehicle that passed. Now it holds only the shape of silence.

inscription: the highway learned: moving is only half of transport; the other half is rest

file.name: Engine_Absence_Ambience_Recording.mp3
file.note: Wind where engines were. Grass where asphalt memorized motion. A recording of a place that forgot how to be driven through.

exits.label: back north to where traffic and wind still debate
south where the horizon goes quiet forever
toward the echo-field where absence has learned to resonate
